title: Neanotis hohenackeri
intro: Neanotis hohenackeri, also known as Hohenacker's Neanotis, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neanotis hohenackeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neanotis hohenackeri is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions, including coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Neanotis hohenackeri is classified within the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Neanotis. The species Neanotis hohenackeri is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant is further classified as Neanotis hohenackeri (Bunge) W.H.Baker in the botanical nomenclature. This classification reflects the species' unique characteristics and its relationships within the Gentianaceae family.
